message:
perf(pager): track live direct-slot indices in AtomicPublishedPages

AtomicPublishedPages carries a fixed array of
ATOMIC_PUBLISHED_SLOT_COUNT (65,535) direct slots but only a handful
are populated at any given moment. Previously every clear(), retain(),
or batch insert over the publication plane walked the entire slot
array and used a Relaxed `present` pre-filter to skip empties. Under
MT-writer contention — which clears the publication plane on every
transaction rollback/retry — the "scan all slots" cost was a
measurable 14.61% self-time at 8 threads on the 2026-04-23 post-T1
capture (fsqlite-mt-post-t1t2t7-184420).

Introduce an `active_indices: Mutex<Vec<usize>>` that tracks the exact
set of direct-slot indices currently holding a published page:

- `insert(page_no, page)` pushes the slot index on a fresh insert and
  leaves it alone on an update-in-place so the vec never duplicates.
- `remove(page_no)` swap_removes the slot from the index list when
  it was present.
- `clear()` drains the index list and touches only those slots (with
  their `present` flag). The `if nothing published, skip scan`
  short-circuit now happens implicitly because drain() over an empty
  Vec is free.
- `retain()` drains the index list, visits only the live slots, and
  rebuilds the list with the retained-subset indices — so the next
  clear() or retain() again touches only live pages.
- A new `insert_batch()` amortizes the Mutex lock across a whole
  batch and is used by PublishedPages::insert_batch to group all
  direct-slot inserts in a single lock acquisition.

A `#[cfg(test)] active_slot_count()` helper exposes the list length,
and a new test `test_published_pages_direct_active_slots_track_live_pages`
asserts the invariant end-to-end: single insert → 1, update in place
→ still 1, second page → 2, remove → 1, clear → 0.

The .beads/issues.jsonl update is sibling bookkeeping from the same
perf tracking epics (bd-db300.2.4, bd-tzlzb, bd-t6sv2.8, bd-wwqen.7,
bd-i9sov) touched while this work was in flight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
